channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: FedEx cargo plane makes emergency landing following bird strike, engine fire
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (AP) — A FedEx cargo plan made an emergency landing at a busy New Jersey airport on Saturday after a bird strike caused an engine fire that could be seen in the morning sky.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump cutoff of humanitarian parole for immigrants from Ukraine, 6 other countries challenged
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — A group of American citizens and immigrants is suing the Trump administration for ending a long-standing legal tool presidents have used to allow people from countries where there's war or political instability to enter and temporarily live in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: GISELA SALOMON, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Kurdish militants declare a ceasefire in a 40-year insurgency in Turkey
item.description: ISTANBUL (AP) — Kurdish militants who have waged a 40-year insurgency in Turkey declared a ceasefire on Saturday in what could mark a significant boost to President Recep Tayyip Erdogan's government, two days after their imprisoned leader called for the group to disarm.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW WILKS, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Thousands of mourners attend funeral of Taliban-linked cleric killed in Pakistan suicide blast
item.description: AKORA KHATTAK, Pakistan (AP) — Thousands of mourners attended a funeral on Saturday for a Taliban-linked cleric slain in a suicide bomb attack on a seminary in Pakistan’s northwest.
item.dc:creator: RIAZ KHAN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Judge blocks Trump order threatening funding for institutions that offer care for transgender youth
item.description: SEATTLE (AP) — President Donald Trump’s plan to pull federal funding from institutions that provide gender-affirming care for transgender youth will remain blocked on a long-term basis under a federal judge's ruling in Seattle late Friday. U.S.
item.dc:creator: GENE JOHNSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: For Gene Hackman, Santa Fe was a refuge from the limelight
item.description: SANTA FE, N.M. (AP) — The piñon- and juniper-scented Rocky Mountain foothills overlooking Santa Fe have helped the city earn a reputation as a refuge for famous actors and authors seeking to escape the spotlight.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Hamas reports no progress in talks with Israel on ceasefire's second phase
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — The latest round of talks on the second phase of the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas has made no progress, and it's unclear whether they will resume on Saturday, a senior Hamas official said.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Barbie celebrates the world of music with 2025 Career of the Year, Alicia Keys doll
item.description: Barbie is celebrating all things music this year, announcing its 2025 Career of the Year and a new doll, highlighting a popular musical artist.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Zelensky won’t apologize for Oval Office spat, says it was bad for both sides
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky wouldn’t apologize for the meeting with President Trump, which erupted into a remarkable and tense spat, arguing that the situation was bad for both countries.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KTLA 5

item.title: Three men charged with plotting Las Vegas bus stop murder
item.description: Three men, Ta Jon Walker, Alexander Hinton, and Devante Reynolds, have been arrested and charged with murder for allegedly plotting the shooting of a man at a bus stop in Las Vegas in 2021.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Bleakley, NewsNation

item.title: Video shows implosion of iconic Six Flags rollercoaster Kingda Ka
item.description: The king has fallen. The legendary Kingda Ka ride at Six Flags Great Adventure in New Jersey was imploded on Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, KRON4

item.title: Tate brothers, who face human trafficking charges in Romania, are back in the US: What to know
item.description: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate, who are charged with human trafficking in Romania, arrived Thursday in the U.S. after authorities lifted travel restrictions on the siblings, who have millions of online followers.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, WGN 9

item.title: New York's governor orders college to remove Palestinian studies job listing
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York's governor ordered a state school to remove a job posting for a Palestinian studies teaching position this week, saying she wanted to ensure “antisemitic theories” would not be taught.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Leland Vittert’s War Notes: Showdown in the Oval
item.description: President Donald Trump and Vice President JD Vance’s double-teaming of Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky in the Oval Office channeled an inner anger among common-sense Americans.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: Peak bloom for the iconic cherry blossom trees of Washington, DC, is predicted for late March
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For decades, the thousands of blooming cherry blossom trees in the nation’s capital have served as both a magnet to visitors and the unofficial start of the tourist season for Washington, D.C.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Firings at US weather and oceans agency risk lives and economy, former agency heads warn
item.description: The people who once ran the federal weather and oceans agency say it touches people’s daily lives in unnoticed ways and that massive firings there could cause needless deaths and a big hit to America’s economy.
item.dc:creator: Seth Borenstein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pennsylvania officer's last act after he was shot was to rescue a colleague, police chief says
item.description: A police officer who was shot to death while responding to an armed man with hostages inside a Pennsylvania hospital intensive care unit was remembered Friday as a dedicated public servant who did not hesitate to run toward danger and died a hero.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Actor Gene Hackman's death not from carbon monoxide, authorities say
item.description: Authorities investigating the deaths of legendary actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa say they have ruled out carbon monoxide poisoning as the cause.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Landlord guilty on all counts in hate crime attack that killed Palestinian American boy, wounded his mother
item.description: Joseph Czuba, 73, faces first-degree murder, attempted murder and hate crime charges in the October 2023 attack that killed Wadee al-Fayoumi, and wounded his mother, Hanan Shaheen.
item.dc:creator: BJ Lutz, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Friday’s 'Planetary Parade' will give you a rare view of the Milky Way
item.description: With the stresses on planet Earth in the headlines, at work and your day-to-day life, do you need a reminder that we're just a speck on a dot in the endless vacuum of space? If so, today's your day.
item.dc:creator: Austin Turner, KTLA 5

item.title: Republicans target SNAP in federal spending cuts; food banks brace for impact
item.description: As House Republicans push to cut billions from the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP), commonly known as food stamps, food banks are bracing for potential impacts.
item.dc:creator: Raquel Martin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Patrolling America's 'third border' from the skies
item.description: The U.S. Coast Guard is taking to the skies to monitor Florida's coast as a crackdown on illegal immigration continues.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: Wyoming's only full-service clinic stops providing abortions after new regulations signed
item.description: CHEYENNE, Wyol (AP) — Wyoming's only full-service abortion clinic stopped providing abortions Friday, but it remained open after Gov. Mark Gordon approved a law requiring that such facilities be licensed as surgical centers.
item.dc:creator: MEAD GRUVER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Microsoft shutting down Skype in May
item.description: Microsoft is closing down Skype, the video-calling service it bought for $8.5 billion in 2011, which had helped spark a transformation in how people communicate online.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Utah is one step away from becoming 1st state to ban fluoride in water
item.description: The Utah governor's signature is all that stands between a state ban on water fluoridation after House Bill 81 cleared its final hurdle in the legislature on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Drachman, WGN 9

item.title: Christianity in US stops decline after years of losses: Survey
item.description: Although down substantially from 2007, 44 percent of Americans said they pray at least once a day, a figure that has remained consistent in the last four years.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Bloody crime scene photos play key role in trial of man accused in Palestinian American boy's death
item.description: JOLIET, Ill. (AP) — Photos of a child's bloody body, a black knife holder and other crime scene evidence took center stage Wednesday at the murder and hate crime trial for an Illinois landlord accused of fatally stabbing a 6-year-old Palestinian American boy in 2023.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIA TAREEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Super Bowl sports reporter found dead in hotel had sedative in his system: authorities
item.description: The woman accused of taking the reporter's phone and bank cards has previously been accused of drugging men to steal from them, according to investigators.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Hamilton, WGN 9

item.title: Police killings set record in 2024, but with smallest increase in years: Report
item.description: In 2024, police officers in the US killed 1,365 people, a slight increase from the 1,329 civilians killed in 2023, according to the Mapping Police Violence report.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Judge criticizes Justice Department's broad reading of Trump's Capitol riot pardons
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge on Wednesday criticized the Justice Department's evolving position that a presidential pardon for a Kentucky man who stormed the Capitol also covers his conviction for illegally possessing guns at his home. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Child becomes first measles death reported in Texas outbreak
item.description: An unvaccinated school-aged child who was hospitalized with measles has passed away, marking the first death in the outbreak, according to Lubbock and state health officials.
item.dc:creator: Caitlyn Rooney, ABC 4

item.title: Computer simulations show nightmare Atlantic current shutdown less likely this century
item.description: The nightmare scenario of Atlantic Ocean currents collapsing, with weather running amok and putting Europe in a deep freeze, looks unlikely this century, a new study concludes.
item.dc:creator: BY SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court to hear arguments on 'reverse discrimination' case
item.description: Marlean Ames filed suit against a state agency claiming she was not given a promotion because she is heterosexual.
item.dc:creator: Safia Samee Ali, ABC 4

item.title: RFK Jr. targets childhood psychiatric drugs; doctors push back
item.description: Childhood psychiatrists insist the drugs, for attention-deficit/hyperactivity disorder (ADHD) and depression, are nonaddictive and proven safe and say they are more concerned about young Americans unable to access psychiatric medications that could help.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, WGN 9

item.title: Adnan Syed's murder conviction still stands as he seeks sentence reduction in 'Serial' case
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Despite documented problems with the evidence against him and an earlier request from prosecutors to clear his record, Adnan Syed will remain convicted of murder, according to court papers filed Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Idaho prosecutors seek to limit Bryan Kohberger's alibi evidence at murder trial
item.description: BOISE, Idaho (AP) — Idaho prosecutors say a man charged in the stabbing deaths of four University of Idaho students shouldn't be allowed to offer an alibi defense unless he takes the stand himself during the murder trial later this year.
item.dc:creator: REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge indefinitely blocks Trump’s plan to freeze federal aid
item.description: A federal judge on Tuesday indefinitely blocked the Trump administration’s freeze of federal funding, dealing a stark blow to President Trump’s sweeping efforts to realign government spending with his agenda. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, WGN 9
